page: Amazon Web Services r5n.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or hardware GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory bandwidth performance, particularly in large-block read and write operations, which rank in the top 25%. Passmark database operations and extended instruction tests also demonstrate strong performance. However, single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng and single-threaded compression benchmarks. Multi-core CPU performance and general Geekbench metrics fall within the average range.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps, supporting high-throughput data transfers. The primary tradeoff of the r5n.16xlarge is its reliance on network-attached EBS storage due to the lack of local SSDs, combined with lower single-threaded compute efficiency. This resource density makes it qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that require high network throughput but do not demand peak single-core CPU speeds. Suitable workloads include large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and multi-threaded data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services r5n.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz. It is configured with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, offering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth and database operations, though single-core compute and compression speeds are weak. Multi-core performance remains average. This profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy, network-intensive applications rather than compute-bound tasks. It is best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and distributed data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 75 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth, optimized for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 75 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high network throughput and dense memory capacity for database and caching workloads.

512.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth and database operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data processing
